message:
fuzz(fcp-raptorq): add envelope decrypt metamorphic fuzz target + 9 tamper corpus seeds

Exercises the SymbolEnvelope::encrypt → decrypt round trip and the
full authenticated-encryption surface (ChaCha20Poly1305 +
XChaCha20Poly1305) against structured per-field tampering. Oracle is
a metamorphic property rather than no-panic: identity mutation must
round-trip the original plaintext byte-for-byte, every other mutation
must be rejected with SymbolEnvelopeError::DecryptFailed — anything
else (success on tampered input, panic, different error variant) is
a fuzz signal.

Input model (Arbitrary + serde-loadable corpus):
- key_seed / zone_key_id / object_id: derived deterministically via
  SHA-256 so the corpus can reproduce crashes without keeping raw
  keys on disk.
- algorithm: ChaCha20Poly1305 | XChaCha20Poly1305.
- mutation: Identity | CiphertextBitflip | CiphertextTruncate |
  AuthTagBitflip | ObjectIdBitflip | KBitflip | EpochIdBitflip |
  EsiBitflip | FrameSeqBitflip | SenderInstanceIdBitflip |
  WrongZoneKey.
- plaintext_len bounded to MAX_PLAINTEXT_LEN = 4096 so per-input
  cost stays inside libfuzzer's default budget.

Mutation mechanics:
- mutation_mask(i) rotates a single bit left — guarantees every
  flip changes exactly one bit, no no-op XORs.
- Ciphertext / object_id / K / epoch_id / ESI / frame_seq /
  sender_instance_id each have a dedicated mutator that flips one
  bit at a mutation_index-derived offset, keeping the rest of the
  envelope structurally valid so decryption exercises the AEAD
  authentication path and not the serde/parse path (which has its
  own fuzz target).
- WrongZoneKey swaps the decrypt key for a derivation with a
  different domain separator; the envelope itself is untouched.

Corpus seeds under fuzz/corpus/fuzz_raptorq_envelope_decrypt/:
- identity_chacha.json: identity round-trip with ChaCha20Poly1305,
  non-zero plaintext, exercises the happy path so crashes on
  encryption itself surface fast.
- tamper_auth_tag.json: flip one bit in the Poly1305 tag, canonical
  AEAD negative test.
- tamper_ciphertext.json: flip one bit in the ciphertext body.
- tamper_epoch_id.json: flip a bit in the epoch id so AAD binding
  diverges.
- tamper_esi.json: flip a bit in the ESI (encoded-symbol index).
- tamper_frame_seq.json: flip a bit in the frame sequence number.
- tamper_k.json: flip a bit in the K parameter (source-block count).
- tamper_sender_instance_id.json: flip a bit in the sender instance.
- wrong_zone_key.json: valid envelope, unrelated zone key on decrypt
  — exercises the key-mismatch path distinctly from bit-flip tampering.

Corpus files are human-readable JSON so they survive schema changes
to EnvelopeDecryptInput (Arbitrary + Deserialize both implemented).

Supporting changes:
- fuzz/Cargo.toml: add the new `fuzz_raptorq_envelope_decrypt` bin
  stanza mirroring the other harnesses (test=false, doc=false,
  bench=false).
- crates/fcp-raptorq/src/lib.rs: re-export SymbolEnvelopeError
  alongside SymbolEnvelope so the fuzz target can name the
  DecryptFailed / EncryptFailed variants via the crate-root path
  (internal variant names don't change).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -304,5 +304,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_raptorq_envelope_decrypt"
+path = "fuzz_targets/raptorq_envelope_decrypt.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
